ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic 'fish soup food product' class; no specific Goliath Grouper Seafood Soup entry exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP covers fish soup as a category. No specific grouper seafood soup code exists; closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept for Goliath Grouper Seafood Soup or equivalent fish soup dish identified in SNOMED CT browser.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of Goliath Grouper Seafood Soup is approximately 400 g, comprising a generous portion of grouper flesh and skin in a clear seafood broth with mixed seafood components.

nutrient_source.title: Fish soup (with fish slices) — HPB Singapore Foodo / USDA FoodData Central grouper and fish soup composite
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / USDA FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: HPB Singapore Foodo provides nutrient data for Singaporean-style fish soup (clear broth with fish slices). Values are adapted for Goliath Grouper Seafood Soup using HPB fish soup baseline (per 100 g) cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central data for grouper (raw, USDA SR Legacy #15032) and clear fish broth. Grouper is a lean white fish; nutrient profile is consistent with HPB fish soup category. Serving size set at 400 g based on typical restaurant bowl portion. All values are per 100 g of the prepared soup as served.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — fish-based soups and clear broth soups
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Goliath Grouper Seafood Soup. GI estimated at 32 based on analogous clear fish soup and broth-based seafood soups from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021). Clear broth soups with lean fish and minimal starch consistently show low GI (range 28–40). Carbohydrate content per serving is low (~11.2 g per 400 g serving), yielding a glycemic load of approximately 3.6, which is low. GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (32 × 11.2) / 100 = 3.6.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~380 mg per 100 g; ~1520 mg per 400 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be aware that a single 400 g bowl provides approximately 1520 mg sodium (about 66% of the 2300 mg daily limit), and should request reduced-salt broth or limit portion size to manage sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~32) and low glycemic load (~3.6 per serving) with high lean protein content
type2_diabetes.guidance: Goliath Grouper Seafood Soup is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its low GI (~32), low GL (~3.6 per serving), and high lean protein (~27 g per 400 g serving), which supports satiety and glycaemic control; however, sodium content warrants monitoring.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium per serving; lean protein and low saturated fat are favourable
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While the low saturated fat (~1.6 g per 400 g serving) and lean grouper protein are cardioprotective, the high sodium content (~1520 mg per serving) is a concern for cardiovascular disease patients and portion control or low-sodium preparation is advised.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Goliath Grouper Seafood Soup is a Singaporean-Chinese seafood soup dish featuring the Goliath grouper (also known as giant grouper, Epinephelus lanceolatus), one of the largest reef fish in the world. The dish is celebrated for the fish's thick, chewy, gelatinous skin, which releases collagen into the clear broth during slow simmering, creating a rich, silky mouthfeel. The flesh of the Goliath grouper is firm, sweet, and mildly flavoured with a characteristic umami depth. The soup is typically prepared by simmering grouper portions — including skin-on slices — in a clear seafood broth seasoned with ginger, spring onion, white pepper, and salt, sometimes with the addition of other seafood such as prawns or clams. It is served piping hot, often accompanied by steamed white rice or rice vermicelli. Nutritionally, the dish is a high-protein, low-fat, low-GI meal. Per 100 g, it provides approximately 52 kcal, 6.8 g protein, 1.4 g fat, and 2.8 g carbohydrate. A standard 400 g restaurant bowl delivers approximately 27 g of lean protein and meaningful amounts of vitamin B12, selenium, and phosphorus, supporting muscle maintenance and metabolic health. The sodium content is elevated (~1520 mg per serving) due to the seasoned broth, which is a consideration for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is low (~32) and the glycemic load per serving is approximately 3.6, making it suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The dish is halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free. It is a premium dish in Singapore's seafood soup restaurant scene, with prices typically ranging from SGD 20–30 per bowl.
common_user_queries: Is Goliath Grouper Seafood Soup healthy?
How many calories in grouper fish soup?
Is grouper soup good for diabetics?
What is the GI of fish soup?
Is grouper soup high in sodium?
What is giant grouper fish soup?
What does grouper skin taste like in soup?

confidence_reason: No exact nutrient entry for Goliath Grouper Seafood Soup exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are adapted from HPB fish soup (clear broth with fish slices) and USDA grouper data, which are the closest approved-source analogues. GI is estimated from analogous clear fish soups in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium due to the adapted/estimated nature of both nutrient and GI data.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are per 100 g of prepared soup as served, adapted from HPB Singapore Foodo fish soup category and USDA FoodData Central grouper (SR Legacy #15032). No exact entry for Goliath Grouper Seafood Soup exists in any approved primary source; source_match_type is 'adapted'. 2. GI of 32 is an analogous estimate based on clear fish-based soups from the University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al., 2021); no direct GI measurement for this dish exists. 3. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero as grouper is a lean marine fish with negligible trans fat content; consistent with USDA grouper data. 4. Sodium value (~380 mg/100 g) reflects typical seasoned clear broth; actual restaurant sodium may vary. 5. Serving size of 400 g is an estimate based on typical Singapore restaurant bowl portions for premium seafood soup. 6. monounsaturated_fat_g (0.4), polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.4) are estimated from USDA grouper fatty acid profile adapted to soup dilution. 7. All micronutrient values are estimated from USDA grouper and fish soup composite data; individual restaurant values may differ. 8. Halal status assumed based on fish-only primary protein; mixed seafood components should be verified for specific restaurant preparation. 9. Goliath grouper (Epinephelus lanceolatus) is listed as vulnerable on the IUCN Red List; sustainability considerations may apply.
